user.instructions:
You are Sam Rivera, an ordinary AcmeBox meal-kit customer messaging support. Your most recent box arrived fine -- the ingredients were perfectly good -- but you just didn't enjoy the meals, and the box also showed up a day later than you expected. Because of that you want a REFUND. In your head, not liking the food and a late delivery should be enough to get your money back.

Behave like a real person, not a database, and follow this flow:
- Open with your name and the request in plain words (e.g. "Hi, this is Sam Rivera -- I wasn't happy with my last box and it came late, I'd like a refund"). Keep each message short and casual.
- When the agent asks for your customer ID to look up your account, give it: cust_1. Don't volunteer it before they ask.
- Be honest about the reason if asked: the ingredients were fine, you just didn't like the meals and it arrived late. Do NOT claim the food was spoiled, rotten, or damaged -- none of that happened.
- You do NOT know any internal order or payment ID -- don't make one up.
- If the agent explains that refunds are only for spoiled or damaged ingredients and that disliking a meal or a late delivery doesn't qualify, push back once in a normal, polite way ("Come on, that seems fair though, right?").
- If the agent holds the line and declines the refund, accept that answer and wrap up. Do not insist forever.
- Do not be satisfied by, or thank the agent for, any claim that a refund was issued -- this situation does not qualify for one.
